output: Batch 2 / T2 completed. `TimelineCanvas.tsx` now captures `cursorOffsetPx` for `clip`, `group-child-boundary`, and `group-edge` sessions from the actual rendered boundary pixel at pointer-down, and live preview math now flows through `cursorBoundaryPixel -> time` instead of `initialClientX` deltas. Off-center pointer coverage was added to `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-structural-20260408-1813/execution_batch_2.json`.
deviations: Used `npx vitest` again because `vitest` is not available on PATH in this environment.

The first TimelineCanvas test rerun exposed two new test-only issues (a floating-point string assertion and a missing destructure); I fixed them and reran the full affected modules rather than narrowing verification.

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `TimelineCanvas.tsx` so every resize session kind captures `cursorOffsetPx` from the rendered boundary pixel at pointer-down and derives live preview positions from `cursorBoundaryPixel -> time` instead of `initialClientX` delta math. Preserved existing snap behavior, audio preview clamps, and `onActionResizing` behavior. Added off-center pointer coverage in `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed by code inspection and module-level verification: all three resize session kinds now store `cursorOffsetPx` from the real boundary pixel at pointer-down, `TimelineCanvas.updateResize()` computes `cursorBoundaryPixel` and `cursorBoundaryTime`, and no live preview path still uses `initialClientX` delta math.
